title: adenophaedra woodsoniana
intro: Adenophaedra woodsoniana, also known as Woodson's Adenophaedra, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. It is native to Madagascar and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving pain, and supporting the immune system, although further research is needed to confirm these effects. Herbal preparations of Adenophaedra woodsoniana are typically made from the leaves and stems, which can be dried and used to make teas, infusions, or tinctures.
